hero.name: Emmanuel Alanis
hero.summary: Platform-level ownership of production systems across fintech and logistics/e-commerce — frontend, backend services, security, and infrastructure. Fluent English, remote-work ready.

stats.stat1: commits shipped across production platforms in the last 5 months
stats.stat2: automated tests maintained across two production platforms
stats.stat4: shipped to production in a 3-week window

about.title: An engineer who owns systems end-to-end.
about.p1: I'm a full-stack developer based in Mexico City. Today I own the React frontend and co-own three backend services for a Mexican SOFOM (consumer lending) — one of two full-stack developers on a small engineering team reporting to the IT director — and I'm a long-term contractor for a US company, building production logistics and e-commerce platforms.
about.p2: I spent years living and working in California, so bilingual means genuinely bicultural: I write specs, run incident reviews, and debate architecture in English without friction.
about.p3: I don't stick to one layer. Features, security hardening, CI/CD, infrastructure — I take responsibility for the whole system, and I use AI tooling (Claude Code, daily) to move faster without cutting corners.

job1.title: Senior Full Stack Developer
job1.desc: Own the React frontend end-to-end and co-own three backend services (Django, Symfony, Node/TypeScript) on a live lending platform, as one of two full-stack developers reporting to the IT director. Majority of the work is production fixes and stabilization — plus acting as the team's merge and release integration point.
job1.b1: Deep specialty in identity verification — INE document OCR, facial liveness detection (MediaPipe), and digital signature flows.
job1.b2: Application security: JWT hardening, MFA and OTP flows, CSP, credential externalization; led a formal security audit with a multi-wave remediation plan.
job1.b3: Led a zero-downtime production migration to AWS; built internal tooling from scratch — storage console, health dashboard, backup browser.

job2.title: Full Stack Engineer (Contractor)
job2.org: Ready Set Group — US client, SF Bay Area
job2.desc: Owns two production platforms end-to-end — features, security hardening, CI/CD, and infrastructure — with daily cross-timezone collaboration in English.
job2.b1: built a partner API integration layer connecting three catering marketplaces — CaterValley (live in production with automated tiered pricing), CaterCow, and ezCater — with HMAC-signed auth and per-partner rate limiting; real-time driver GPS tracking with proof-of-delivery capture; PostGIS-based mileage calculation for payroll; migrated infrastructure off Vercel to self-managed Docker on a Hetzner VPS.
job2.b2: built two-way Square catalog sync that eliminated double data entry for restaurant staff, a concurrency-safe checkout preventing double charges and duplicate orders, resilience engineering (circuit breakers, retry queues), and a serious QA rig — Playwright E2E, accessibility testing, load testing — shipping 14 production releases in 3 weeks.

p1.title: Consumer Lending Platform — Mexican SOFOM
p1.desc: A loan-origination platform with biometric KYC and digital signing — evidence of owning a compliance-heavy, security-critical production system at scale.
p1.b1: Facial liveness detection and INE document verification

p2.title: Ready Set — Logistics & Delivery Platform
p2.desc: Multi-sided platform connecting vendors, drivers, and dispatch for on-demand delivery. Live partner marketplace integrations (CaterValley, CaterCow, ezCater), real-time GPS tracking, a native driver-app proof-of-concept, and a self-hosted infra migration.

p3.title: Destino SF — E-commerce & Catering Platform
p3.desc: Storefront and catering system for a restaurant, with two-way Square catalog sync and concurrency-safe checkout. Resilience engineering — circuit breakers, retry queues — plus rigorous QA (Playwright, accessibility, load testing) and a rapid release cadence.

blog.title: Notes from production.
blog.intro: Writing about web development, new technologies, and the practices that hold up in real production systems.

featured.title: Zero-downtime AWS migrations: a practical checklist
featured.desc: What it actually takes to move a live lending platform to AWS without a maintenance window — DNS strategy, dual-writes, and the rollback plan nobody wants to use.
featured.descShort: What it actually takes to move a live lending platform to AWS without a maintenance window.
